taskDescription: Drag the correct terms into the blanks to complete statements about enzymes.
textField:
The protein part of an enzyme without its cofactor is called the *apoenzyme*, while the complete catalytically active enzyme is called the *holoenzyme*.

In Michaelis-Menten kinetics, the *Km* represents the substrate concentration at which the reaction velocity is half of *Vmax*.

A *competitive* inhibitor binds to the active site and increases the apparent Km without changing Vmax. A *non-competitive* inhibitor binds to an allosteric site and decreases Vmax without changing Km.

The six classes of enzymes are oxidoreductases, transferases, *hydrolases*, *lyases*, isomerases, and *ligases*.

Serum *troponin* is the most specific cardiac marker used to diagnose myocardial infarction.

Enzymes lower the *activation* energy of a reaction without altering the equilibrium constant.

The lock-and-key model was proposed by *Fischer*, while the induced-fit model was proposed by Koshland.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review enzyme concepts and try again.
Good effort! Review the areas you missed.
